---
title: "Korelasi"
author: "Amelia Widiastuti (5052241007)"
date: "2026-04-19"
output: html_document
---

---

# 1. Load Library & Data

```{r load}
library(tidyverse)
library(corrplot)
library(ggcorrplot)
library(psych)

df <- read.csv("clean_data.csv")
head(df)
```

```{r struktur}
str(df)
summary(df)
```

Pilih kolom numerik:

```{r seleksi}
df_num <- df %>% select(age, salary, performance_score, years_experience)
head(df_num)
```


---

# 2. Pearson Correlation

```{r}
cor_pearson <- function(a, b) {
  n    <- length(a)
  mean_a <- mean(a)
  mean_b <- mean(b)

  num   <- 0
  denom_a <- 0
  denom_b <- 0

  for (i in 1:n) {
    num     <- num     + (a[i] - mean_a) * (b[i] - mean_b)
    denom_a <- denom_a + (a[i] - mean_a)^2
    denom_b <- denom_b + (b[i] - mean_b)^2
  }

  return(num / sqrt(denom_a * denom_b))
}
```

```{r}
target_vars <- c("age", "years_experience", "performance_score")

cat("Pearson (manual) vs salary\n\n")
for (v in target_vars) {
  r <- cor_pearson(df_num[[v]], df_num$salary)
  cat(sprintf("  %-22s | r = %.4f\n", v, r))
}
```

Uji signifikansi terhadap `salary` untuk setiap variabel prediktor:

```{r}
for (v in target_vars) {
  cat("Pearson:", v, "vs salary\n")
  result <- cor.test(df_num[[v]], df_num$salary, method = "pearson")
  cat(sprintf("    r = %.3f | p-value = %.4f\n\n", result$estimate, result$p.value))
}
```

---

# 3. Spearman Correlation

```{r def-spearman}
cor_spearman <- function(a, b) {
  n      <- length(a)
  rank_a <- rank(a)
  rank_b <- rank(b)

  sum_d2 <- 0
  for (i in 1:n) {
    d       <- rank_a[i] - rank_b[i]
    sum_d2  <- sum_d2 + d^2
  }

  return(1 - (6 * sum_d2) / (n * (n^2 - 1)))
}
```

```{r}
cat("Spearman (manual) vs salary\n\n")
for (v in target_vars) {
  rho <- cor_spearman(df_num[[v]], df_num$salary)
  cat(sprintf("  %-22s | rho = %.4f\n", v, rho))
}
```

```{r}
for (v in target_vars) {
  cat("Spearman:", v, "vs salary\n")
  result <- cor.test(df_num[[v]], df_num$salary, method = "spearman")
  cat(sprintf("    rho = %.3f | p-value = %.4f\n\n", result$estimate, result$p.value))
}
```

---

# 4. Kendall Tau

```{r}
cor_kendall <- function(a, b) {
  n <- length(a)
  C <- 0
  D <- 0

  for (i in 1:(n - 1)) {
    for (j in (i + 1):n) {
      sign_a <- sign(a[j] - a[i])
      sign_b <- sign(b[j] - b[i])
      prod   <- sign_a * sign_b

      if (prod > 0) C <- C + 1
      if (prod < 0) D <- D + 1
      # prod == 0 tidak dihitung
    }
  }

  return((C - D) / (0.5 * n * (n - 1)))
}
```

```{r}
cat("Kendall (manual) vs salary\n\n")
for (v in target_vars) {
  tau <- cor_kendall(df_num[[v]], df_num$salary)
  cat(sprintf("  %-22s | tau = %.4f\n", v, tau))
}
```

```{r}
for (v in target_vars) {
  cat("Kendall:", v, "vs salary\n")
  result <- cor.test(df_num[[v]], df_num$salary, method = "kendall")
  cat(sprintf("    tau = %.3f | p-value = %.4f\n\n", result$estimate, result$p.value))
}
```

---

# 5. Perbandingan Ketiga Metode

```{r}
cor_funcs <- list(
  Pearson  = cor_pearson,
  Spearman = cor_spearman,
  Kendall  = cor_kendall
)

hasil <- lapply(cor_funcs, function(fn) {
  sapply(target_vars, function(v) round(fn(df_num[[v]], df_num$salary), 3))
})

tabel <- as.data.frame(do.call(rbind, hasil))
tabel <- cbind(Metode = names(cor_funcs), tabel)
colnames(tabel) <- c("Metode", paste0(target_vars, " × salary"))
rownames(tabel) <- NULL

tabel
```

---

# 6. Uji Normalitas (Syarat Pearson)

```{r normalitas}
all_vars <- c("salary", target_vars)

for (v in all_vars) {
  result <- shapiro.test(df_num[[v]])
  status <- ifelse(result$p.value < 0.05, "TIDAK normal ❌", "Normal ✅")
  cat(sprintf("%-22s | W = %.4f | p-value = %.4f | %s\n",
              v, result$statistic, result$p.value, status))
}
```

> Jika p-value < 0.05 → data **tidak normal** → gunakan **Spearman** atau **Kendall**

---

# 7. Kesimpulan

```{r kesimpulan}
cat("Ringkasan Korelasi dengan Salary\n\n")
cat(sprintf("%-22s | %8s | %8s | %8s\n", "Variabel", "Pearson", "Spearman", "Kendall"))
cat(strrep("-", 58), "\n")

for (v in target_vars) {
  r_p <- round(cor_pearson(df_num[[v]],  df_num$salary), 3)
  r_s <- round(cor_spearman(df_num[[v]], df_num$salary), 3)
  r_k <- round(cor_kendall(df_num[[v]],  df_num$salary), 3)
  cat(sprintf("%-22s | %8.3f | %8.3f | %8.3f\n", v, r_p, r_s, r_k))
}
```
